name: SOL Range Failure Reacceptance v1
thesis: SOL 4h is the best-fit primary lane for a range-failure reversal after a multi-bar breakout loses acceptance and re-enters balance. In a balanced chop/transition tape, the edge should come from trapped continuation flow unwinding back into value rather than from trying to catch exact tops and bottoms.
invalidation_condition: Abort if range-failure setups remain too sparse, if reacceptance does not rotate price back through value, or if the lane trends too cleanly for reversal logic.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price extends beyond a local range for more than one bar, loses acceptance, and rotates back through near-term value as trapped continuation participants unwind.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: With chop and transition evenly represented, failed continuation should remain common. Re-entry into balance is broader and denser than waiting for rare exhaustion candles.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h is suitable because false acceptance after visible expansion is clear enough to define balance loss and reacceptance without needing intraday noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If SOL enters persistent trend rather than failed continuation, reversal entries will be steamrolled.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If reversal participation exists but exits too early, widen the back-half exit before tightening the reacceptance trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; extension context and reacceptance are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed with broad multi-bar extension plus value re-entry instead of exact trap candles

long: Trail remaining 20% behind EMA_20 with 0.4 * ATR_14 buffer
short: Trail remaining 20% behind EMA_20 with 0.4 * ATR_14 buffer
